safety_alerts.message: Fermented preparations (Asava/Arishta) contain naturally occurring alcohol from fermentation. These are generally unsuitable in Prameha (Diabetes) as alcohol can cause blood sugar fluctuations. Classical texts advise caution. Use with expert supervision only.
safety_alerts.classical_basis: Charaka Samhita, Chikitsasthana — Asava/Arishta contraindicated in Prameha context

safety_alerts.message: This formulation contains a sweetener ingredient (Guda/Sharkara/Mishri). Sweeteners are generally contraindicated in Prameha/Diabetes due to their Madhura (sweet) taste which may aggravate the condition.
safety_alerts.classical_basis: Madhura rasa contraindicated in Prameha — Charaka Samhita, Nidanasthana 4

references.excerpt_text: Aqueous decoction — suitable in Prameha context as it contains no fermented ingredients or sweeteners.

explanation.summary: Vyaghryadi Kashayam is an aqueous decoction (Kwatha) — safe for use in Prameha as it contains no fermented alcohol or sweeteners. Its Kantakari, Shunthi, Pippali ingredients are Ushna-Katu in nature and suitable for respiratory presentations.

safety_violations.message: Fermented preparations (Asava/Arishta) contain naturally occurring alcohol from fermentation. Generally unsuitable in Prameha (Diabetes). Use with expert supervision only.
safety_violations.classical_basis: Charaka Samhita, Chikitsasthana — Asava/Arishta contraindicated in Prameha context

references.excerpt_text: Amritarishta — fermented preparation indicated in all types of fever.

explanation.summary: Amritarishta is indicated for Jvara but carries a WARN safety flag for Prameha (Diabetes) due to its fermented nature (Asava/Arishta class). Use only under expert supervision.

disclaimer: Educational decision support only. Not a diagnosis or prescription. Clinical judgment of a qualified vaidya is required.
